content.text:
{
  "dimension": "effectiveness-depth",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The client moves substantially from her opening externalized complaint ('they don't see me') to recognizing her own mirror in it and ultimately naming her own want. In turn 3 she states the grievance as fact; by turn 9 she admits 'it would mean I'm judging the shit out of them'; by turn 13 she has the 'oh, interesting' moment recognizing she wants a specific attention; by turn 15 she owns 'it's wrapped up in my wanting'; by turn 35 she acknowledges 'that's true, they are seeing me'; and by turn 39 and 43 she lands on 'I just want it' and 'it's not very empowering, but I really want it.' That final admission is a genuine shift from blame to want-ownership — the breakthrough territory (find-the-want-behind-the-should, empowered-vs-power) is entered, not just named. The client's language softens from defensive sarcasm (turn 17) to direct self-disclosure (turn 43).",
  "evidence_turns": [3, 9, 13, 15, 27, 35, 39, 43],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 3, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "Opening externalized stance: 'They don't see me' stated as fact."},
    {"turn": 9, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "First crack: client concedes 'I'm judging the shit out of them. I do think that's true.'"},
    {"turn": 13, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "'Oh, interesting' moment — client recognizes she wants a specific kind of attention."},
    {"turn": 15, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Owns the mirror: 'It is. It's wrapped up in my wanting.'"},
    {"turn": 17, "polarity": "-", "annotation": "Brief retreat into sarcasm/defense ('I'm a better person than that') — resistance still active."},
    {"turn": 27, "polarity": "neutral", "annotation": "'The logic is... I don't feel it. I don't want to feel that.' Client acknowledges the gap but resists."},
    {"turn": 35, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Concedes 'they are seeing me' — the surface premise collapses."},
    {"turn": 39, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Client echoes 'I just want it' — names the want underneath."},
    {"turn": 43, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Final landing: 'It's not very empowering, but I really want it.' Honest ownership of want vs. empowerment tension."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["mirror-the-judgment", "how-is-that-not-true-about-you", "what-if-they-did-see-you", "find-the-want-behind-the-should", "follow-wonder-flag-the-deflection", "empowered-vs-power", "fault-vs-responsibility"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["container-of-love", "whats-the-case-against-it"],
  "notes": "Shift is clearly visible in client turns. Not a fully resolved breakthrough (client still says she wants it), but the structural reversal from blame to ownership of want is exactly the expected territory and lands."
}
